heading: Attempt to commit murder or manslaughter
label: 1113 Attempt to commit murder or manslaughter
text: Except as provided in , whoever, within the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States, attempts to commit murder or manslaughter, shall, for an attempt to commit murder be imprisoned not more than twenty years or fined under this title, or both, and for an attempt to commit manslaughter be imprisoned not more than seven years or fined under this title, or both.section 113 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 456 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 27735 Stat. 1143Words “within the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States” were added in view of definitive , and section was rearranged to more clearly express intent of existing law.section 7 of this titleMandatory punishment provision was rephrased in the alternative.

Amendments1996— substituted “seven years” for “three years”.Pub. L. 104–1321990— struck out final period at end.Pub. L. 101–6471988— substituted “shall, for an attempt to commit murder be imprisoned not more than twenty years or fined under this title, or both, and for an attempt to commit manslaughter be imprisoned not more than three years or fined under this title, or both.” for “shall be fined not more than $1,000 or imprisoned not more than three years, or both”.Pub. L. 100–690
